structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Air Distribution Effectiveness Determination (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Determine Ez: if DAT ≤ space temperature, Ez = EzC (default 1.0). If DAT > space temperature, Ez = EzH (default 0.8).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Calculation when zone ventilation values are determined.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ez equals EzC or EzH accordingly.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. Determine zone air distribution effectiveness Ez . 1. If the DAT at the terminal unit is less than or equal to zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzC (d efault to 1.0 if no value is scheduled). 2. If the DAT at the terminal unit is greater than zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzH (default to 0.8 if no value is scheduled).

evidence_quote: b. Determine zone air distribution effectiveness Ez . 1. If the DAT at the terminal unit is less than or equal to zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzC (d efault to 1.0 if no value is scheduled). 2. If the DAT at the terminal unit is greater than zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzH (default to 0.8 if no value is scheduled).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Airflow Vmin Calculation (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If Vmin is set to 'AUTO', Vmin equals Voz when AHU has been supplying 100% OA for 10 minutes, else equals 1.5 * Voz. Otherwise Vmin is as entered.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Continuous evaluation; Vmin updates based on AHU OA condition.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: d. Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes; 2. Else shall be equal to 1.5 * Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler is not supplying 100% outdoor air; 3. Else shall be equal Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2 .

evidence_quote: d. Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes; 2. Else shall be equal to 1.5 * Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler is not supplying 100% outdoor air; 3. Else shall be equal Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Outdoor Airflow Calculation and Priority Overrides (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Priority overrides: 1) if zone not occupied or window open => Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0; 2) if occupancy sensor unpopulated and occupied-standby allowed => same zero; 3) if unpopulated but not allowed => Vbz-P*=0, Vmin*=Vmin.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Based on zone mode, window switch, occupancy sensor status.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority: 1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0. 2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 . 3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin.

evidence_quote: f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority: 1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0. 2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 . 3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Demand Controlled Ventilation Loop (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, P-only loop maintains CO2 at setpoint, reset 0% at setpoint-200 ppm to 100% at setpoint. Loop disabled outside Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone in Occupied Mode with CO2 sensor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ii. During Occupied Mode , a P-only loop shall maintain CO 2 concentration at setpoint ; reset from 0% at setpoint  minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint . iii. Loop is disabled and outp ut set to zero when the zone is not in Occupied Mode .

evidence_quote: ii. During Occupied Mode , a P-only loop shall maintain CO 2 concentration at setpoint ; reset from 0% at setpoint  minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint . iii. Loop is disabled and outp ut set to zero when the zone is not in Occupied Mode .

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset for Cooling-Only/Reheat/Series Fan/Dual-Duct Terminal Units (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Cooling-only VAV, reheat VAV, constant-volume series fan-powered, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and airflow sensors, dual-duct with discharge airflow sensor, or dual-duct with cold-duct minimum control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: CO2 loop output resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to Vcool-max at 100%, and Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P at 100%, in parallel.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone with CO2 sensor and Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset Vmin* and Vbz-P* linearly with loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset for Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Units (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: VCO2-max = Vcool-max when cooling, else Vcool-max minus parallel fan airflow. CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to VCO2-max at 100%, and Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P at 100%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone with CO2 sensor and Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: v. For parallel fan -powered terminal units: (a) Determine VCO2 -max as follows: (1) When the Zone State  is cooling, VCO2 -max is equal to the maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max. (2) When the Zone State  is heating or deadband , VCO2 -max is equal to Vcool -max minus the parallel fan airflow This logic prevents the total supply airflow from exceeding Vcool-max, which could create diffuser noise problems. (b) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin* and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  VCO2 -max at 100% loop outp ut. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output. See Figure 5.2.1.3 -2.

evidence_quote: v. For parallel fan -powered terminal units: (a) Determine VCO2 -max as follows: (1) When the Zone State  is cooling, VCO2 -max is equal to the maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max. (2) When the Zone State  is heating or deadband , VCO2 -max is equal to Vcool -max minus the parallel fan airflow This logic prevents the total supply airflow from exceeding Vcool-max, which could create diffuser noise problems. (b) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin* and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  VCO2 -max at 100% loop outp ut. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output. See Figure 5.2.1.3 -2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset for Single-Zone VAV AHUs (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: MinOAsp = Voz. CO2 loop resets Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P at 100% loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone with CO2 sensor and Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: vi. For SZVAV AHUs : (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint  MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the  population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz -P at 100% loop output. See Figure 5.2.1.3 -3.

evidence_quote: vi. For SZVAV AHUs : (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint  MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the  population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz -P at 100% loop output. See Figure 5.2.1.3 -3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air Setpoints Calculation (California Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone-Abs-OA-min reset: zero if window switch open; zero if occupancy sensor unpopulated and occupied-standby allowed; Varea-min if CO2 sensor; else Zone-Des-OA-min. Zone-Des-OA-min: zero if window switch open; zero if unpopulated and occupied-standby allowed; else larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. Determine the zone minimum outdoor air setpoint s Zone -Abs-OA-min and Zone -Des-OA-min. ... 1. Zone -Abs-OA-min shall be reset based on the following conditions in order from highest  to lowest  priority: i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and th e window is open . ii. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor and is unpopulated  and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 . iii. Varea -min if the zone has a CO 2 sensor . iv. Zone -Des-OA-min otherwise . 2. Zone -Des-OA-min is equal to the following , in order from highest to lowest priority : i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open . ii. Zero if the zone has an occupanc y sensor , is unpopulated , and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 . iii. The larger of Varea -min and Vocc -min otherwise .

evidence_quote: b. Determine the zone minimum outdoor air setpoint s Zone -Abs-OA-min and Zone -Des-OA-min. ... 1. Zone -Abs-OA-min shall be reset based on the following conditions in order from highest  to lowest  priority: i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and th e window is open . ii. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor and is unpopulated  and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 . iii. Varea -min if the zone has a CO 2 sensor . iv. Zone -Des-OA-min otherwise . 2. Zone -Des-OA-min is equal to the following , in order from highest to lowest priority : i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open . ii. Zero if the zone has an occupanc y sensor , is unpopulated , and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 . iii. The larger of Varea -min and Vocc -min otherwise .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Airflow Vmin Calculation (Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If Vmin is 'AUTO', Vmin = Zone-Abs-OA-min; else Vmin as entered.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Zone -Abs-OA-min if Vmin in Section 3.1.2  is “AUTO”; 2. Else shall be equal to Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2 .

evidence_quote: c. Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Zone -Abs-OA-min if Vmin in Section 3.1.2  is “AUTO”; 2. Else shall be equal to Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupied Minimum Airflow Override Priorities (Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Vmin* = Vmin except: zero if occupancy sensor unpopulated and occupied-standby allowed; zero if window switch open.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Occupancy sensor or window switch status.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: d. The occupied minimum airflow Vmin* shall be equal to Vmin except as noted below , in order from highest to lowest priority : 1. If the zone has an occupancy sensor  and is permitted to be in occup ied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 , Vmin* shall be equal to zero when the room is unpopulated. 2. If the zone has a window switch, Vmin* shall be  zero when the window is open.

evidence_quote: d. The occupied minimum airflow Vmin* shall be equal to Vmin except as noted below , in order from highest to lowest priority : 1. If the zone has an occupancy sensor  and is permitted to be in occup ied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 , Vmin* shall be equal to zero when the room is unpopulated. 2. If the zone has a window switch, Vmin* shall be  zero when the window is open.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset for Cooling-Only/Reheat/Series Fan/Dual-Duct Under Title 24
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Cooling-only VAV, reheat VAV, constant-volume series fan-powered, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and airflow sensors, dual-duct with discharge airflow sensor, or dual-duct with cold-duct minimum control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to Vcool-max at 50% loop output; loop output 50-100% used for system-level OA reset.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone with CO2 sensor in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max at 50%, as shown in Figure 5.2.1.4 -1. The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.

evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint  Vcool -max at 50%, as shown in Figure 5.2.1.4 -1. The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.

The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: vi. For SZVAV AHUs : (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint  MinOAsp shall be reset based on the zone CO2 control -loop signal from MinOA  at 0% signal to DesOA  at 100% signal. See Figure 5.2.1.4 -3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) Pulse Width Modulation Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm (lowest controllable), apply TAV: TAVratio = Vspt/Vm; TCT = 15 min adjustable; open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT*TAVratio), Vspt* = Vm; closed period CP = TCT - OP, Vspt* = 0, damper loop disabled output 0, at end command damper to last position before reenabling; on entry, initial open period = RNDM*OP, RNDM random 0-1.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.2.2.1. When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint  allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint  shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation ( TAV ) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period . During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint  Vspt * shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of  the following : d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period.  During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper con trol loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling  the control loop. g. During TAV mode, e ach cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm. h. When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0 .

evidence_quote: 5.2.2.1. When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint  allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint  shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation ( TAV ) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period . During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint  Vspt * shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of  the following : d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period.  During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper con trol loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling  the control loop. g. During TAV mode, e ach cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm. h. When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Airflow Setpoint Override in TAV Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint Vspt is overridden to Vspt*.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.2.2.2. When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt* .

evidence_quote: 5.2.2.2. When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt* .

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Sensor Fault Detection Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarm if CO2 < 300 ppm, or zone unoccupied >2 hours and CO2 > 600 ppm (Level 3, sensor may be out of calibration). Alarm if CO2 exceeds setpoint+10% for >10 min (Level 3).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode  for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration. b. If the CO 2 concentration exceeds setpoint  plus 10% for more than 10 minute s, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode  for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration. b. If the CO 2 concentration exceeds setpoint  plus 10% for more than 10 minute s, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Selection Between Ventilation Compliance Standards
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Engineer must select: for ASHRAE 62.1 compliance, use Section 5.2.1.3 and delete 5.2.1.4; for California Title 24, use Section 5.2.1.4 and delete 5.2.1.3.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The engineer must select between ventilation logic options: If the project is to comply with ASHRAE Standard 62.1  ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.3  and delete Section 5.2.1.4 . If the project is to comply with California Title 24 ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.4  and delete Section 5.2.1.3 .

evidence_quote: The engineer must select between ventilation logic options: If the project is to comply with ASHRAE Standard 62.1  ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.3  and delete Section 5.2.1.4 . If the project is to comply with California Title 24 ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.4  and delete Section 5.2.1.3 .
